Set `app.config['COMPRESS_MIN_SIZE'] = 500` to skip compressing tiny responses. Set `app.config['COMPRESS_ALGORITHM'] = 'gzip'` for widest client compatibility (also supports 'br' for Brotli if the library is available). Exclude already-compressed content types from the default list: 'image/png', 'image/jpeg', 'application/pdf'. Verify the Content-Encoding header is set correctly in responses by checking with curl: `curl -H 'Accept-Encoding: gzip' -v http://localhost:5001/api/gtm/scenarios`.

tasks.title: ETag/caching for scenario data
tasks.description: Add HTTP caching headers to GET endpoints in backend/demo_app.py that serve static or semi-static data. For scenario endpoints (`/api/gtm/scenarios`, `/api/gtm/scenarios/<id>`, `/api/gtm/seed-data/<type>`): compute an ETag by MD5-hashing the JSON response body (`hashlib.md5(json_body.encode()).hexdigest()`). Before returning the response, check the request's `If-None-Match` header — if it matches the computed ETag, return 304 Not Modified with no body. Otherwise, return the full response with `ETag` and `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600` headers. For dynamic endpoints (simulation status, report data, chat), set `Cache-Control: no-store` to prevent caching. Create a decorator `@with_etag` that wraps endpoint functions and handles the ETag computation and 304 logic automatically. Apply it to all scenario and graph data endpoints. For the frontend static files served via the production nginx config, add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` for hashed assets and `Cache-Control: no-cache` for index.html.

tasks.title: API versioning (/api/v1/)
tasks.description: Add API version prefixing to all endpoints in backend/demo_app.py. Use Flask's Blueprint feature: create a Blueprint `api_v1 = Blueprint('api_v1', __name__, url_prefix='/api/v1')` and move all existing route handlers to it. Register the blueprint on the app. Add a redirect from `/api/*` to `/api/v1/*` for backwards compatibility using `@app.route('/api/<path:path>')` that returns a 301 redirect. Add an `X-API-Version: v1` header to all responses using `@api_v1.after_request`. Update the health endpoint to include `api_version: 'v1'` in its response. Document the versioning strategy in a comment block: future breaking changes will be introduced under `/api/v2/` while `/api/v1/` remains stable. Update the frontend API client (`frontend/src/api/client.js`) to use the `/api/v1/` prefix in the baseURL configuration. Ensure the VITE_API_URL env var and the docker-compose proxy configuration are updated to reflect the new path.

tasks.title: Graceful shutdown handling
tasks.description: Add signal handlers for graceful shutdown in backend/demo_app.py. Import `signal` and `sys`. Register handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT (Ctrl+C) that: 1) Log 'Shutdown signal received, starting graceful shutdown...', 2) Set a module-level `_shutting_down = True` flag, 3) Add a `@app.before_request` check that returns 503 Service Unavailable with `Retry-After: 30` header when `_shutting_down` is True (stop accepting new requests), 4) Wait up to 30 seconds for in-progress requests to complete (track active requests with a threading counter incremented in before_request and decremented in after_request), 5) Clean up simulation state — log summary of active simulations being abandoned, 6) Log 'Shutdown complete. Served N total requests, M active at shutdown.' and call `sys.exit(0)`. For gunicorn, also add a `worker_exit` hook in a gunicorn config file (`backend/gunicorn.conf.py`) that runs the same cleanup. This ensures Docker containers stop cleanly without orphaned connections.

tasks.title: Background task cleanup
tasks.description: Add periodic cleanup for stale in-memory simulation data in backend/demo_app.py. The module-level dicts `_graph_tasks`, `_simulations`, and `_reports` grow unbounded as the app runs. Create a cleanup function `_cleanup_stale_data()` that: iterates through each dict, removes entries where `time.time() - entry['start'] > 3600` (1 hour old), and logs the count of removed entries. Run this cleanup in two ways: 1) Lazily — check and clean up at the start of every request (only if last cleanup was > 5 minutes ago, tracked via a `_last_cleanup` timestamp). 2) On-demand — add a POST endpoint `/api/admin/cleanup` that triggers immediate cleanup and returns `{removed: {graph_tasks: N, simulations: N, reports: N}, remaining: {graph_tasks: N, simulations: N, reports: N}}`. Also clean up any other memory accumulators added by earlier tasks (shared_reports tokens, annotations, token usage stats if they grow too large). Log cleanup actions with counts at INFO level.

tasks.title: Rate limiting middleware
tasks.description: Add per-IP rate limiting to backend/demo_app.py. Implement a simple in-memory rate limiter without external dependencies (no Redis needed for this demo). Create a `RateLimiter` class that tracks request counts per IP address using a dict of `{ip: [{timestamp}, ...]}` with a sliding window. Define three rate limit tiers configured via env vars: `RATE_LIMIT_API` (default 100 requests/minute for general API endpoints), `RATE_LIMIT_LLM` (default 10 requests/minute for LLM-powered endpoints like /chat and /generate-seed), and `RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT` (default 5 requests/minute for export endpoints like PDF export). Apply via `@app.before_request`: determine the tier from the request path, check the rate limit, and if exceeded, return 429 Too Many Requests with a JSON body `{error: 'Rate limit exceeded', retry_after: N}` and `Retry-After` header. Clean up old entries (> 1 minute) on each check to prevent memory growth. Exempt /api/health from rate limiting.

tasks.title: Favicon + OG meta tags
tasks.description: Add proper favicon and Open Graph meta tags to the frontend. In `frontend/public/`, create favicon files: generate a simple favicon using the Intercom blue (#2068FF) as background with a white 'M' letter or fish icon — create as SVG first, then generate favicon.ico (16x16, 32x32 multi-size), apple-touch-icon.png (180x180), and favicon-32x32.png. Update `frontend/index.html` to include: `<link rel='icon' href='/favicon.ico'>`, `<link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/apple-touch-icon.png'>`, `<link rel='icon' type='image/png' sizes='32x32' href='/favicon-32x32.png'>`. Add OG meta tags: `<meta property='og:title' content='MiroFish GTM Demo'>`, `<meta property='og:description' content='Swarm intelligence simulation for GTM strategy'>`, `<meta property='og:type' content='website'>`, `<meta property='og:image' content='/og-image.png'>` (create a 1200x630 OG image placeholder). Add Twitter card meta tags: `<meta name='twitter:card' content='summary_large_image'>`. Add theme-color: `<meta name='theme-color' content='#2068FF'>`.

tasks.title: Onboarding tour for first-time visitors
tasks.description: Create a first-visit onboarding experience in `frontend/src/components/demo/OnboardingOverlay.vue`. Check localStorage for a `mirofish_onboarded` flag — if not set, show the onboarding on the landing page. Render as a full-screen semi-transparent overlay with a centered card sequence (not the guided tour spotlight — this is a simpler welcome flow). Show 3-4 cards in a horizontal carousel/stepper: Card 1: 'Welcome to MiroFish' with a brief description and the MiroFish logo. Card 2: 'Simulate buyer behavior' — explain that AI agents model real personas. Card 3: 'Get actionable insights' — explain reports and chat. Card 4: 'Get Started' CTA button that dismisses the overlay. Add step indicators (dots) at the bottom. Support swiping on mobile and arrow key navigation on desktop. Add a 'Skip' link in the top-right corner. On completion or skip, set `mirofish_onboarded = true` in localStorage. Add a 'Restart Onboarding' option in SettingsView.vue that clears the flag. Mount the component in LandingView.vue conditionally.

tasks.title: Parallax hero scroll effect
tasks.description: Add a subtle parallax scrolling effect to the hero section of frontend/src/views/LandingView.vue. The landing page already has a dark hero section with gradient background. Enhance it by adding 2-3 background layers that scroll at different speeds relative to the main content. Layer 1 (farthest): the existing HeroSwarm.vue particle animation — move at 0.3x scroll speed. Layer 2 (middle): subtle grid dots or geometric shapes — move at 0.5x scroll speed. Layer 3 (foreground): the text content — scrolls normally at 1x. Implement using `requestAnimationFrame` and a scroll event listener: on each frame, read `window.scrollY` and apply `transform: translateY(${scrollY * rate}px)` to each layer. Important: check for `prefers-reduced-motion` media query and disable all parallax if the user prefers reduced motion (`window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches`). Also disable on mobile (viewport width < 768px) to preserve performance. Keep the effect subtle — maximum parallax offset should be ~50px.

tasks.title: Smooth page transitions
tasks.description: Add smooth animated transitions between all route changes in the Vue app. Wrap the `<RouterView>` in App.vue (or AppLayout.vue) with Vue's `<Transition>` component. Detect navigation direction by comparing the route's meta.depth or history index: forward navigation (deeper in the app) uses a slide-left + fade-in animation, backward navigation uses slide-right + fade-in. Define CSS transition classes: `.page-enter-active, .page-leave-active { transition: all 300ms ease; }`, `.page-enter-from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(20px); }`, `.page-leave-to { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(-20px); }` (reverse for backward). Add route meta `depth` values: landing=0, scenarios=1, workspace=2, report=3, chat=4, settings=1. Use `<RouterView v-slot='{ Component, route }'>` pattern with dynamic transition name based on direction. Ensure the transition doesn't cause layout jumps — use `position: relative` on the router-view container and `position: absolute` on entering/leaving elements during transition. Add `will-change: transform, opacity` for GPU acceleration.

tasks.title: Micro-interaction animations
tasks.description: Add subtle, delightful micro-animations throughout the frontend. In `frontend/src/assets/brand-tokens.css` or a new `animations.css` file, define reusable animation classes: `.hover-lift` (on hover: translateY(-2px) + subtle box-shadow increase, 150ms ease), `.hover-scale` (on hover: scale(1.02), 100ms ease), `.focus-glow` (on focus: box-shadow with 0 0 0 3px rgba(32,104,255,0.3)), `.click-press` (on active: scale(0.98), 50ms), `.slide-in-right` (translateX(20px) → 0, 200ms), `.fade-up` (translateY(10px) + opacity 0 → 1, 200ms). Apply these to existing components: Button.vue gets hover-scale and click-press, Card.vue gets hover-lift, Input.vue gets focus-glow, notification toasts get slide-in-right, metric cards get a number count-up pulse animation on value change. Add a CSS `@keyframes pulse` for data point hover in charts (scale 1 → 1.3 → 1 over 300ms). Ensure all animations respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` by wrapping in a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` block.

tasks.title: Final visual QA brand consistency pass
tasks.description: Perform a comprehensive visual audit of every view and component in the frontend. Create a QA checklist file at `docs/visual-qa-checklist.md` and verify each item: 1) All primary action buttons use Intercom blue (#2068FF) with white text, 2) All destructive action buttons use red (#dc2626), 3) Typography follows the type scale defined in brand-tokens.css — headings use tight letter-spacing, body text uses system-ui font, 4) Spacing follows an 8px grid (padding and margins are multiples of 8px: 8, 16, 24, 32, 40, 48), 5) Border radius is consistent: cards use rounded-lg (0.5rem), buttons use rounded-md (0.375rem), inputs use rounded-md, 6) Icon sizes are uniform: 16px inline, 20px in buttons, 24px standalone, 7) Dark mode renders correctly on every page — no white-on-white or black-on-black text, 8) All charts use brand colors consistently, 9) No broken layouts at viewport widths: 375px (iPhone), 768px (iPad), 1024px (laptop), 1440px (desktop), 10) Cards have consistent border treatment (border-black/10 in light, border-white/10 in dark). Fix any inconsistencies found during the audit.
